reviews.review_description:
Wow! This has become one of my favourite VR games recently!
On flat screen I wasn't really interested, but I thought I would give it a try on VR. I'm so glad that I did! This is way better than I expected and very addictive intriguing narrative structure.
There is no combat, but it doesn't need it. The script is so engaging, the different tasks are challenging in a fun way, the voice acting is brilliant, the art design is full of life!
Now the graphics aren't as realistic on VR but from what I've seen on flat screen of it, it do think I prefer the colour schemes of the VR version. The palette gives such a shine to the game that really works.
Dialogue choices and timed responses aren't actually that worrying, because it keeps you engaged and it's thrilling wanting to know where it's leading and how your choices affect matters.
The atmosphere is incredible! The start has a big jump scare, but after that the horror is more tense than jump scares, the sound really helps build that atmosphere so that you feel right in it. It perfectly balances purposeful humour and hamminess, alongside serious and severe themes. It perfectly parodies slasher flicks of old.
It's a cool idea setting it in the 80s. Even though the people you talk to shows that everyone must have mobile phones, which weren't a thing. But that is only a silly issue with the game because it helps it work, that you have to assist people who call you. You don't see the action taking place, but the dialogue makes sure that you don't have to. It encourages your imagination in the best ways!

The puzzles and tasks are never too taxing but they are clever to make you feel like you're actually using your head and solving issues on your own. As long as you pay attention, focus, get all relevant info and think logically then you should be able to solve everything! Sometimes it scares you that you've made the wrong choice but then you find out that it was tricking you. Stick with it! You won't be disappointed!
There are so many branching storylines that keep things intriguing. But challenge yourself to see if you can manage to save everyone and get that rare achievement!
The game isn't too long but also isn't short. They make full use of environment and what is on offer.
It didn't end as I expected, but the ending is still very satisfying, especially if you solve everything.

Everyone should check this game out! We need more quality narrative games like this that work so well in VR! We need to support this! You won't regret it!

If you are okay with being mostly stationary while solving puzzles to save the residents of a small town, this is the game for you. Not many jump scares (I can only think of two), and it's more the vibe of the game that I loved.
 
As a kid of the 80's/90's, the graphics, music etc. certainly have that great nostalgic feeling that I love. 

I highly recommend this if this sounds like a game for you. 

EDIT: I didn't realize originally that there was more than one ending! Now I'm really obsessed.

If you loved Choose Your Own Adventure books as a kid, this game is exactly that! Choices you make not only impact the characters you're trying to save from The Whistling Man, it impacts the final outcome as well! I've finished it twice now with different endings...the second time went a little better 😉

I hope everyone enjoys this game as much as I did, and I'm going to leave it installed on my VR headset because I have a feeling I'll come back to this game for a third go at it...
If you want to feel like you’re back in the 80s this is the game. Art style is perfect and story line is good. I haven’t finished it yet but what I played I was blown away.

I really enjoyed this game. Some really good puzzles, some more difficult than others and 2 different outcomes depending on how you get on with the puzzles (I died). Couple of little things that got me tho was that u can’t repeat anything Peggy tells u, like during the fire and u have to choose who’s closest to save the caller…. Pay attention cos u can’t repeat the information. And at the end I didn’t get a chance to pick up and item before the consequences (don’t want to say to much and spoil the ending) but it definitely impacted my ending. 
Other than that amazing game. Will there be another?
way to much of dialogues reading,  very little (and very easy) gameplay
the game is amazing. the voice acting, the story, the animations, the models, the interaction, everything is amazing!!! i love this game even though didn't finish it

I just got done playing this game and all i have to say is, wow. This game just hit the spot for the game i have been looking for. I got this game for free through meta horizon plus but i would have bought it. If you want a puzzle game with a little bit of horror as you solve mysteries then this is the game for you. It has you solving puzzles and making logical and moral decisions as you try to help save the callers from the town serial killer. And i recommend not reading the reviews that are very low because they all say they were looking for a normal run and kill the monster horror game but if you watch the trailer, it is clearly not that. The next passage contains SPOILERS so do not read unless you want to.





The game provides the best horror experience for those who want to be scared but not jumpscared and for those who want an impactful story. The game tries to make you think you will see the killer in the station by making the most creepy atmosphere possible but later you realize it is your safe space, until the killer breaks in. You do not run from him but you do hear him then see him through the glass windows. This games runs for 3 to 5 hours and i totally recommend it for people who want this type of experience.

I’m honestly here to gush. This game was incredible. Killer Frequency is special from its head to its toes. This piece of art showcases the incredible use-cases of VR in capturing an immersive horror scenario that will leave your heart RACING. 

If you’re over the age of 35, or well-versed in all things 1980s, the environment and atmosphere will be a treat. The developers took their time fine-tuning the experience paying incredibly close attention to small details and it SHOWS. Understand that I’m a gaming critic, but I genuinely struggled to find any bugs in this title. It’s well DONE. 

The voice acting in this game was honestly next level. The character development and lore is there in abundance for anyone with a curious eye. 

This game is also very replayable as events unfold based on your decision making. With almost 5 hours of run time per play through, the many, many different endings make this a title I’ll keep coming back to for awhile. 

Buy this game.

This was a fun romp in 80s slasher movie nostalgia. A very interesting take and way to present it.  Plenty of content and creepy stuff. The story had me guessing till the end and you guessed it, a big twist. Only complaint was some wonky typical VR jank that was minimal and not a huge deal. Biggest thing was height at the console. I had to constantly crouch to reach buttons and when I set it to work, the 1st time I had to crouch, it thew it all off again. The other gripe was snap turning. Come on guys.... snap turning only. Geesh. Even with that, a well worth the play game.

A well written horror story with talented voice actors sprinkled with the right amount of scary. Never felt lost nor pampered during the puzzles.
Great work!

Some nitpicks for the devs:
 * You need to add a height scaling on standing mode, I "bumped" my head on every doorframe. My workaround was to play seated, set artificial seated on/off and it have fortunately reset my seated height
* With snap turning, it was impossible to be seated exactly in front of the panel. Long pressing the meta button did not work as it should. Workaround of course is to rotate IRL
* Dialog boxes look awfully sterile. They take up most of the interactions and while they have that wavy effect sometimes, it would have been great to be overall visually pleasing/fitting

I had to give it 3 stars instead of 5. This was a fantastic game, loved everything about it..it was a little glitchy at times but still a great game and story. But the frequent uses of GD was just too much. I feel it isn't called for in games, esp with kids playing it. I would've played again because I killed a lot of ppl making the wrong choices but I can't get past the cussing.
I love the concept of this game and the setting, characters, and story. Its like a movie!

Most one star reviews critique this game on its lack of chase scenes or direct contact with the killer, but that’s what makes this game unique. Rather than be the one being hunted, it’s all in your hands to save the actual prey. It’s scary not because you may die, but because one slip up, and you just let someone get killed. The story, characters, and art style all fit together to bring a unique retro horror game that leaves you wondering for days after you finish.
Wonderful game with a great narrative approach and just enough tension to keep you guessing.
I just want to let you know I got this on horizon + ( which you should get) but DO NOT get this game if you get scared easily trust me I almost shot my pants on the tutorial.

reviews.review_title: I am traumitized in a good way...
reviews.review_description:
So i've seen this game all of my meta store hidden this whole time so once i realized it was on meta horizon+, i said eh, i'll give it a try so i launched it, understood what i was supposed to do and holy shoot i am crying on the side and not only laughing on the outside but shaking.
crazy game
Such an underrated game❤️
I thought YOU became the killer lol

reviews.review_title: An honest review, just buy the game!
reviews.review_description: This game is incredible! I love it to pieces I love the thrill and joy of helping people, it may be boring to mainly sit in a room but it’s so fun and if you’re thinking of buying it, just do it honestly.
3 different endings 2 good and 1 bad, all depends on who u let die. Would love a sequel.
Tried it. Listened for almost 1hours. Still waiting for the puzzles…. There was only conversations. Nothing to do 😢
Game has problems with height all the time. Can't make it playable while seated, and standing all the time is just...eh
amazing story in plot it was scary and all the right ways there wasn't really any jump scares but it was creepy and the amazing sort of way
Most of the game is js listening to people speak and then you get very few chances for options to pick

boring. 

Team 17 did this? God, what I wouldn't give for a classic Team 17 Amiga shooter about now. I'd at least wake up!

I understand the 1 and 2 star reviews, I get it is boring to sit in a room most of the time and just answer questions, but that's the point of the game, a horror puzzle game, and i loved the story, how the killers were somehow related to Peggy, how there's different endings, and I love how 1 of the mysterious callers turns out to be a killer, and I love how you figure out there's 2 killers in the end.
I love horror games, however I also like to sit back and do a puzzle, so this was one of my favourite VR games I've ever played, and might even play it for a 3rd time.

I really hope we get a Killer Frequency 2, even though its unlikely, I'd like to learn more about Peggy and her sister, and I'd love to play as her aswell!

You should put seated options in the settings menu, not after the crouch tutorial.
It's basically a lot of taking this game is.. Yeah it's a story game mostly point and click and the voice acting is great but it's still boring.

Also soo buggy! For some reason my right hand stopped working completely but only in game, the controller works fine everywhere else.

It's just not for me I get board sitting still waiting for ppl to stop talking.
I wish there was smooth turning
for some reason im really tall idk why
Peak
This is a great vr game and it is also available for purchase on playstation and X-box

reviews.review_description: Good
Horrible game
The intro in the ally gave me a heart attack
This is an amazing game to play with lore story and different ways options to pick this is amazing also GIVE US KF 2
A amazing game
OK, I'm in the early stages of the game and so far it's pretty good! Really looking forward to seeing how it develops.
the only what i can say was WoW🥲
I havent played this in vr yet but played the pc version I loved it
Perfect game, great story and great intuitive gameplay
Very interesting story and I love the graphics. I really wish they made a seated option with all of the dialogue in the game. Seems like a missed opportunity.

When this first cam out I loved the concept then I forgot about it then I remembered and bought this game for vr and man was it worth it this game was amazing the puzzles are so well done the story beside the killer is so interesting and keeps you on your toes I love the qurikey characters it’s just good give this game a shot it’s worth every penny and every kill I’ve been your host forest Nash and your reading 189.16 the scream goodbye caller
I've been playing this so far and I am enjoying it, I love how every action you do feels so suspenseful and overall is a Great game

￼ This is by far one of the coolest things I’ve experienced in VR from being a radio host to be immersed in a very creepy setting in a small town this game just feels great all around cool story, cool dialogue and overall a very fun experience. I hope the devs behind this one continue to bring us more titles like this. Definitely a must play on the quest in my opinion. ￼

Lots of talking but a fun game so far. I was stuck in crouch mode for a while, which was annoying as I never selected a crouch. But enjoying the atmosphere so far. The radio station is a bit creepier than I thought it would be, especially outside of the main room!

reviews.review_title: Too much talking for my ADHD ahhh
reviews.review_description: With ADHD there was way too much talking, but still I had a blast playing recommended for new users going into scary VR franchises as there is not many scary parts, but still the thrill of having a life at steak.
Only 1 hour in and already enjoying it. I like the branching dialogues and stressful situations and decisions. Makes me feel like I’m playing The Walking Dead again. I like the 80s aesthetic too.

This genuinely is one of the best vr titles I’ve ever played, great down to the TEE. Super suspenseful, your choices actually mean something in the end. Great character writing and more. HOPING for more stuff like this
I liked the story line very much, great game, but there could be much more interactions, but still pretty good game i would recommend to play.

Nice idea, but unfortunately not very engaging: there's way too much talking and all the situations are completely unrealistic – the emergency call is diverted to the radio and all conversations happen on air; you first have to look up phone numbers while people are being chased by the killer or bleeding out. And I just can’t figure out how people in the pre-cellphone era were always able to get on the phone.
In the first playthrough, 14 out of 22 people were rescued, and I had no desire to try it again, if only because you can’t skip the conversations.
I guess you have to play with save-scumming right from the start.

This game has been one of the best experiences I had in VR. The voice acting and the story is brilliant, and the gameplay elements are great as well. I recommend it for anyone who likes puzzle games and 90s horror movies.

I want to give you a warning! If you enjoy action or shooter games and dislike story-driven puzzle games, this game may not be for you. However, if you appreciate a well-crafted narrative, you will likely love this game as much as I did! It’s unlike anything I’ve ever played. The game is dialog-heavy, which perfectly complements the story and overall style. While it can be a bit scary at times, it doesn't contain gore or excessive jump scares. 

You play as a radio operator working the night shift, starting around midnight and finishing by 4 AM, which aligns well with the game's length—another positive aspect. 

Depending on which characters you choose to save, you’ll uncover different backgrounds in the story. The more people you save, the longer the game becomes. I’m already eager to play it again. Now that I know the correct answers, I’ll have to wait until my memory fades before trying again. Even better, I hope the developers create a similar game or a sequel!
I LOVE THIS GAME YOU CAN COLLECT SOUVENIRS FROM EACH ENCOUNTER
Very fun, suspenseful,  and interesting storyline

reviews.review_title: This game is so dang fun
reviews.review_description: It’s got the perfect 80’s aesthetic throughout the entire game, and the story is a 10/10. Playing the game itself is very immersive with how you run the radio show, and the scary parts really keep you on your feet. Killer Frequency is a great game to play if you like 80’s music and slasher movies, as it’s a perfect mix of both. The ending song is so perfect for the game.

reviews.review_title: I hope they make a sequel!
reviews.review_description: Great game! I was eyeballing this for awhile but finally decided to get it and i regret not getting it earlier. I love thriller games but i don’t like all the jump scares it comes with. This game was perfect for me. It was eerie and i wasn’t sure if i was going to run into a jump scare but luckily there aren’t that many of them. I really hope they make a second part to this. The only con that i do have is that the dialogues can be really long. Especially when you have to play an ad in the game. I wish that there was an option to speed through it. I played a second round of it to try and save some of the people i didn’t the first time so an option to be able to fast forward the dialogue would have been great.
Lame replica of keep talking none explode, barely no gamepaly and full of premature puzzles

reviews.review_title: They never fixed a common issue!
reviews.review_description: I really enjoyed what I could play of the game. There is a part in the first 25% of the game where you have to go get a record and play it. When you do the producer gets stuck in a loop and keeps telling me to play the record. Even when I do she keeps telling me to play the record so the game will NOT move forward. I have looked online and this has been a very common issue that they (the developers) said they will fix it but that was almost two years ago and I just played it so obviously they did NOT fix it! I have restarted 3 times and it just keeps happening. Tried to get a refund but I was past the 14 day timeframe so I’m SOL!

1. The locomotion sucks.
2. No height adjustment setting.
3. and overall just clunky movement. ￼

I've been wanting to get the game for a while, so just now decided to grab it.
From what I've played so far, the atmodphere, writing/ dialogue, and voice acting is absolutely superb! The humor is great without seeming forced, cartoony, or otherwise distracting.
The method of "puzzles" via dialogue  hoices ftom what little I've played is also great. Something new and captivating next to my many other puzzle titles.
The one thing, ONE thing, that drives me mad and drops this otherwise seemingly 5 Star game down a few notches is the controls.
What I mean by that is the FORCED "Snap" turning with no other options for camera movement outside of the degrees the turns snap...
There's zero reason any game should have this as the ONLY option, and makes me think we are lucky to even have Smooth locomotion (thank God, as I very well may have just requested a refund without it...).

So a great game let down by the camera control if all things..
Not for me. Boring af, can’t do smooth turns, only snap turn allowed, the only good thing is great graphics and atmosphere. Wasted money, unfortunately…

Really?? No smooth turn?

What a joke.  Makes for a very uncomfortable experience, instantly returning.

I love this game along with the idea 
Me and my friend played it and we laughed, got scared and a bit angry when it came to teddy 
 Love this game five stars
It fun it’s horr

I wish I would have read all the 1 star reviews before I bought it . This is the stupidest game you literally have to listen to fake ads! What a waste of money Im so mad at myself for getting sucked in to the fake 5 star reviews.

To open movement settings I must use the left joystick, to move I must also use the left joystick, my left joystick has controller drift which I'll admit it sucks, but I would please like a refund, I want my money back, or at least some of it, please
I am an hour in and nothing happens. All “action” seems to happen via conversation. Incredibly boring.

Ok, at first when I got this game I saw a really bad review saying that it glitched a lot and that this isn’t as good as other games, but then I realized that they probably had bad internet and well… this ISN’T like other games (like I expect you to die or something) this is BETTER than other games. Also looking at the trailer I thought that all the gameplay is saving that jazz runner girl and Maurice, but oh my god this is so much more. (Also sometimes even though the main focus is saving everyone I just end up trying to throw paper balls into the trash can 🤣) anyways I don’t really have anymore room to say anything else but I just wanted everyone to know this is a good ga-             Edit: OH MY GOD THIS IS SO AMAZING PLEASE MAKE A SECOND ONE AND TO MAKE IT EASIER PLEASE KEEP THE SETTING AND ALSO KEEP FOREST AND PEGGY AS THE MAINS also has anyone ever been super like sad or something when you finish a game even though there are multiple endings and you haven’t gotten them all so you could actually keep playing

now, ill say im not the bestest when it comes to playing vr horror games considering i have a weak heart- and i shake pretty easily, but with killer frequency? jesus christ this was amazing and wonderful, i always felt so on edge when entering the basement with the tapes because i thought id get jumpscared, so i closed my eyes half the time. when i was listening to the tapes and heard the ambience inside the background with the doors closing i went still instantly and held my breath, i have a literal recording of only forrests hands just shaking slightly and staring the at the recorder. i loved killer frequency, and the characters!! eugene was a hard one, aswell as maurice(who i may or may have not gotten killed) and i LOVED the plot. 

this game was absolutely fantastic, i loved it so much and i’ll definitely be trying to persuade my friends to play it aswell. 

(ps. henry barrows is a cutie patootie)

This game is really fun, and can be extremely creepy. To all those that say 5 star reviews are "paid", no, they just get the entire premise of the game. Maybe try at least watching the trailer for the game or reading the description before buying it. Anyways, amazing work Team 17, hope for another amazing game on 189.16, The Scream

After playing both Red Matter 1&2 and I expect you to die 1-3, I was looking for another great game to dive into. This was NOT it. I bought it and it glitched like crazy. Controls don’t make any sense and are hard to use. And the opening “practice” through the alley was sooooo pointless, considering you’re in a radio station the whole time. So I requested a refund.I thought this would be up there with RM and IEYTD, but it is farrrr from it. Today I decided maybe I cut it off too soon, so I repurchased it to see if it got better and give it a chance. Nope! I sat there for nearly an hour just listening to the annoying dj and peggy and answering questions that were unnecessary since it doesn’t matter what answer you click. The dialogue is boring. The “jokes” aren’t funny and I had to restart the game twice due to freezing and glitching. I literally dozed off in my chair listening to this game. And of course, now I’m not eligible for a refund due to me “playing” the game for 2 hours. When literally I did nothing but listen to banter between the characters. This game is worth $10 max. Now I’m out $25 for having high hopes and giving it another try. Disappointing. The high reviews are crazy. Find another game to invest $25 in. This ain’t it.

I love this games whole story building narrative which really puts you in the town, even the little jokes help add to it. Especially when you leave the radio setup your whole feeling of safety goes away and you feel really alone. Also all the fun things you can mess with while listening to the people talk makes the story a little more engaging and less of a book. This game is super underrated I highly recommend to anybody unless you are highly sensitive to scary stuff (there are some moments) Anyways great game would love a sequel.

Wow. This game is excellent thus far! I’ve only played about 2 hours of it and I thoroughly enjoyed it. Definitely creepy and a little scary, but I’m also a huge wimp with even mild horror or suspense. The puzzles are easy, and I only lost Miss Jazzical because I was lazy, lol.

Graphics fit the genre perfectly- retro 80s with a hint of erie detail.

Characters- 9.5/10
Voice acting- 10/10
Storytelling- 9/10
Design- 10/10
Gameplay-8.5/10 (likely just a personal issue)


(Personal) fear factor- 7/10
(Average person) fear factor- 4/10

I definitely recommend buying this game!
I LOVED this game. I wish they would build on it and open up the map so you can explore the town. I did not want to stop playing once it was over. Easy 5 stars

Hello and welcome to 189.16 the scream
I just finished playing Killer Frequency for vr 
I totally recommend it it's also on PC ps5/4 and switch and I think Xbox
It's a really good horror game that has you playing as a late night host but the police are out of town looking for help you are in charge of saving those being chased by the whistling man 
It was definitely Worth the wait from the original game on Game jolt and I'm so happy it was this good hopefully a dlc or something comes out to see the point of view of the whistling man and not just hear about it

reviews.review_title: Paid Reviews? Awful awful terrible game
reviews.review_description:
If anyone out there is trying to make a serial killer game where you actually have to play a game where you run from a serial killer, please, do NOT let this game slow you down in that effort. It’s certainly not done in this game. You’ve got an open market and people like me WANT THAT GAME.  

 This game started so promising. Serial killer scare and then I got into the “start” And started hiding and crouching and waiting to have to beat up a serial killer. 

And nothing. It’s a closed station!!! All you do is answer phone calls and “play” “puzzles” That are poorly constructed.  and otherwise answer SIM questions that literally make no difference in how you answer them. 

I went from being excited beyond comprehension to being angry. It was insane letdown. And I agree with other reviewers who question whether all those positive reviews were from paid people. No way anyone thought these “puzzles” were stimulating. Absolutely no way.

I don’t know what these positive reviews are talking about.

I’ve been playing on the Oculus Quest 2 for two years and played dozens of games. This feels like a game made over two years ago.

90% of the game is standing in one place repeating the same actions over and over between long tedious expositions. The other 10% is going from place A to place B, pick up item, and go back to Place A. The few puzzles are not really well thought out and the few times that you do decide on an action it feels inconsequential. 

The graphics are below average. The controls are clunky. It’s hard to grab and view things. The environment is mostly static and flat.

That’s the good parts.

$26 was too much- this feels like a $10 interactive experience. And worth passing on.

reviews.review_title: Looks fun and I am about to play!
reviews.review_description:
I'm giving this five stars because so far it seems really good. I haven't finished it yet. But I also wanted to share and hope this helps! :)

reviews.review_title: The other reviews must have been paid
reviews.review_description: I REALLY do not understand how this game received so many good reviews! I am SO MAD and I feel played. I really wish I could get my money back! There is no reason why this game should have so many good reviews! You literally sit there the entire time not doing anything but answering questions! There is no actual game play! In the very beginning it tricks you because the tutorial has you walking in an ally and then a jump scare which is really promising…. But then you end up at the radio show, in front of your turn table equipment, not moving at all. You answer phone calls and direct the people on the other line how to survive. You can’t even see it! You’re in the radio show! You see a screen and the caller will say like “Oh no! He’s coming!” And you’ll have three things to choose from like “Run” or “Hide” or “Swim” (for example). It’s the most boring “game” I’ve ever played and I’m soooooo mad that I spent that much money on it!!! I waited a LONG time for something to happen too! Lame and idk if you can tell but I’m MADDDDDDD because the reviews lied and I feel played!
